question.text: GST is applicable on which components of derivative trading costs?

answer.text: GST at 18% is applicable on brokerage, exchange transaction charges, and SEBI turnover fees — it is NOT applicable on STT, stamp duty, or the derivative transaction itself
GST is applicable on the derivative transaction value itself at 5%
No GST applies to any component of derivative trading
GST at 28% applies to all derivative trading costs including STT

explanation.text: **The Logic:** GST (18%) is levied on service components: brokerage charged by the broker, exchange transaction charges, and SEBI turnover fees. STT (a tax) and stamp duty are excluded from GST. The derivative trade itself is not a supply of goods or services for GST purposes. **The Trap:** GST does NOT apply to the derivative transaction value or to government taxes (STT, stamp duty). It applies only to the service fees (brokerage, exchange charges). **Cross-Exam Context:** GST applicability is the same across all derivative segments — currency, interest rate, equity, and commodity. Series XVI Ch9 explicitly mentions 18% GST on brokerage. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** GST on brokerage is an additional cost that many retail traders overlook. Factor it into your total cost calculation: Brokerage + 18% GST on brokerage + STT + Stamp duty + Exchange charges + 18% GST on exchange charges.
